assertions.description: L1 uses symbol 'DIN-DIO-Relay-controller-rescue:L_Core_Ferrite-Device' which has 'Ferrite' in the name from a KiCad library rescue. However L1 is a 10uH shielded power inductor (LCSC C5349663, footprint Inductor_4.2x4.4mm) used as the switching element in the TPS61040 boost converter. The statistics section reports 'ferrite_bead: 1' and no inductor count. The BOM also lists L1 as an inductor with footprint 'Inductor_4.2x4.4mm'. The analyzer should classify components by value/footprint context when the symbol lib_id is from a rescue library with misleading names.

The analyzer uses assumed_vref=0.6 (heuristic) for U1 (TPS61040DBV), computing estimated_vout = 0.6*(1 + 240k/13k) = 11.677V. The TPS61040 datasheet specifies Vref=0.5V, so actual Vout = 0.5*(1 + 240k/13k) ≈ 9.73V. OpenTherm boiler protocol operates at 15-24V bus, and this 24V boost from 5V VCC is used to drive the boiler interface side. The ~20% error in Vout estimation (11.7V vs 9.7V) is caused by the heuristic Vref being too high. The TPS61040 lib_id 'Regulator_Switching:TPS61040DBV' is a known part that could be looked up for the correct 0.5V reference.

The design uses U2 and U3 (LTV-817S, lib_id=Isolator:LTV-817S) as galvanic isolation barriers between the boiler/OpenTherm side (+24V domain) and the Arduino side (VCC domain). This is the core purpose of the OpenTherm shield. The signal_analysis.isolation_barriers array is empty, missing both optocouplers. They are classified only as generic 'ic' and appear only in transistor_circuits (as base_driver_ics for Q1/Q5). The cross_domain_signals section correctly identifies that SLAVE IN crosses power domains and 'needs_level_shifter: true', confirming the analyzer sees isolation is needed but fails to attribute it to U2/U3.

Three Zener diodes are used for voltage clamping/protection on the OpenTherm interface signal lines: D6 (4.7V zener, lib_id=archive:D_Zener_ALT-Device), D7 (13V zener), and D8 (4.3V zener). These protect against transients from the boiler bus. The signal_analysis.protection_devices array is empty, and the diodes are classified only as generic 'diode' type in statistics. Zener diodes with lib_ids containing 'Zener' in their symbol name should be detected as protection devices.
